description_long: This mud patch is an irregularly shaped section of earth, featuring a rough, uneven surface with visible clumps of soil and patches of moss or grass. The coloration ranges from dark brown to gray, with hints of green from organic growth along the upper edges. The texture appears coarse and granular, with some areas showing compacted dirt and others more loosely aggregated. The boundaries are jagged and blend into the surrounding terrain, suggesting a natural formation rather than a man-made one. There are no clear signs of water accumulation, but the surface looks slightly moist, indicating recent exposure to damp conditions. Scattered bits of organic debris, such as small leaves and moss, are visible on the surface.
description: An irregular, coarse mud patch with clumps of soil, moss, and organic debris, featuring a rough, moist surface and jagged edges.
description_view_0: A broad, flat face of the mud patch with visible soil clumps, moss, and a rough, uneven surface.
description_view_1: A narrow, edge-on view highlighting the thinness and irregular vertical profile of the mud patch.
description_view_2: A wide, horizontal view showing the patch's length, rough surface, and some deeper soil textures.
description_view_3: Another edge-on perspective, emphasizing the jagged, uneven boundary and thin profile.
attribute_location: urban park pathway or roadside verge
attribute_boundary_definition: The boundaries are irregular and blend gradually into the surrounding ground, with no sharp transitions between mud and adjacent surfaces.
